content: I'm so excited - I just adopted a golden retriever named Finn from a shelter! He's 2 years old and already house-trained.
Congratulations! That's amazing news. How's the adjustment been so far?
It's been great, actually. Finn is such a sweet boy, and he's already settled in really well. I've been taking him on lots of walks and playing fetch with him in the park.
That sounds like so much fun! As a pediatrician, you must be used to working with kids, but how's it been adjusting to having a furry child?

Ha! Yeah, it's definitely different, but I love it. I've been listening to a lot of podcasts about dog training and behavior, and it's been really helpful. I've also been trying to get Finn to join me on my hikes and runs.
That's great that you're prioritizing Finn's training and exercise. Do you have any fun plans for the two of you in the coming months?

Actually, I'm thinking of signing us up for a dog-friendly hiking trip in the mountains. Maybe we'll even try some backpacking trips next summer. And in the meantime, I've been trying to get into beekeeping - I've got a friend who's been teaching me the basics.
That sounds like an amazing adventure! You'll have to be sure to pack plenty of snacks and water for Finn. Have you thought about how you'll balance your work schedule with taking care of Finn?

Yeah, I've been thinking about that a lot. I'm lucky to have a pretty flexible schedule at the clinic, so I should be able to take care of Finn's needs. And my colleague, Dr. Lee, has been really understanding about my new responsibilities as a dog owner.
That's great to hear. It's always important to have a supportive team. How's Finn adjusting to your work schedule - does he get anxious when you leave for work?

Actually, he's been doing really well. I've been leaving him with a dog walker or a friend when I'm at work, and he seems to be happy and relaxed when I get home. Maybe I'll even start a podcast about my experiences as a dog owner and pediatrician.
That's a great idea! You could share your knowledge and expertise with others. Have you thought about what you'll do if Finn needs any medical care or attention?

Yeah, I've been doing some research on local vets and pet hospitals. I've also been thinking about getting pet insurance to be on the safe side. And I've been trying to get Finn to join me on my marathon training runs - he loves running alongside me.
That's great that you're being proactive about Finn's health. It's always better to be prepared. I'm sure Finn will love going on runs with you - he's probably getting plenty of exercise already.
Definitely. I'm just trying to make sure I can give him the best life possible. Thanks for chatting with me - it's been great to talk about Finn and my experiences as a dog owner.
It was great talking to you too! I'm glad I could help you process your thoughts and feelings about being a dog owner.
